---
title: "Non Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease & Fibrosis Transcriptomics"
subtitle: "BNFO 420 Capstone"
---

## Abstract 
Non Alcoholic Liver Disease(NAFLD) is a condition where fat is accumulated in the
liver. This disease can cause inflammation, fibrosis, and cirrhosis. Fibrosis is 
characterized by excessive deposition of extracellular matrix (ECM) components, 
leading to structural and functional disruption of liver tissue. Understanding 
the molecular mechanisms underlying fibrosis progression is critical for identifying
therapeutic targets and biomarkers of disease severity. In this study, we analyzed 
RNA-sequence data from the GSE135251 dataset. This dataset consists of liver biopsy 
samples from 216 human patients, classified as control, early, and moderate. We 
hypothesized that as fibrosis stages increases, expression of genes related to 
extracellular matrix organization would be increased. To test this hypothesis, the 
raw counts data was processed using DESeq2 to identify differentially expressed genes 
between early and moderate fibrosis. Principal component analysis(PCA) was used to asses 
global Transcriptomic differences accross the samples. Differential expression results were visualized 
using a volcano plot and heatmap of top significant genes. Gene Ontology(GO) enrichment analysis
was performedd using clusterProfiler to identify overrepresented biological processes.
Our analysis reaveled distinct transcriptomic differences between control and fibrosis samples, with 
more subtle differences found between early and moderate stages. Pathway enrichment analysis revealeled 
significant upregulation of genes associated with extracellular matrix organization in moderate fibrosis. 
These findings support the hypothesis that fibrosis progression is 


## Introduction


Write 4–6 sentences describing:
- the problem or question
- the dataset(s)
- your approach (methods)
- what you will deliver (figures, model, dashboard, etc.)

## Project roadmap (living)
- [ ] Our dataset: GEO GSE135251
- [ ] Define outcome + predictors / variables
- [ ] Exploratory analysis + QC
- [ ] Method 1 (required)
- [ ] Method 2 (optional/extension)
- [ ] Final figures + interpretation
- [ ] Limitations + ethics + reproducibility notes

## Where to find things
- **Scripts:** `scripts/`
- **Data notes:** `data/README.md`
- **Figures:** `figures/`
- **Citations:** `references.bib`
